content.content:
-- $Id: testes/gc.lua $
-- See Copyright Notice in file all.lua

print('testing incremental garbage collection')

local debug = require"debug"

assert(collectgarbage("isrunning"))

collectgarbage()

local oldmode = collectgarbage("incremental")

-- changing modes should return previous mode
assert(collectgarbage("generational") == "incremental")
assert(collectgarbage("generational") == "generational")
assert(collectgarbage("incremental") == "generational")
assert(collectgarbage("incremental") == "incremental")


local function nop () end

local function gcinfo ()
  return collectgarbage"count" * 1024
end


-- test weird parameters to 'collectgarbage'
do
  -- save original parameters
  local a = collectgarbage("setpause", 200)
  local b = collectgarbage("setstepmul", 200)
  local t = {0, 2, 10, 90, 500, 5000, 30000, 0x7ffffffe}
  for i = 1, #t do
    local p = t[i]
    for j = 1, #t do
      local m = t[j]
      collectgarbage("setpause", p)
      collectgarbage("setstepmul", m)
      collectgarbage("step", 0)
      collectgarbage("step", 10000)
    end
  end
  -- restore original parameters
  collectgarbage("setpause", a)
  collectgarbage("setstepmul", b)
  collectgarbage()
end

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

tool_use_result.stdout:

# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole

# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1

        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

content.content:
3:use crate::closure::LuaClosure;
18:    Function(LuaClosure),
283:            LuaClosure::Lua(x)    => Rc::strong_count(&x.0) == 1,
284:            LuaClosure::C(x)      => Rc::strong_count(&x.0) == 1,
285:            LuaClosure::LightC(_) => false,
291:fn closure_eq(a: &LuaClosure, b: &LuaClosure) -> bool {
293:        (LuaClosure::Lua(x), LuaClosure::Lua(y)) => GcRef::ptr_eq(x, y),
294:        (LuaClosure::C(x), LuaClosure::C(y)) => GcRef::ptr_eq(x, y),
295:        (LuaClosure::LightC(x), LuaClosure::LightC(y)) => x == y,

delta.text: I found the bug. In `crates/lua-types/src/value.rs
`, `weakly_held_alone` checks `Rc::strong_count == 1`
, but for an entry where the key and value are the same `Rc` (e.g., `a[t] = t`), the count is 2 even with no external references, so the entry
is incorrectly kept. Let me fix this.

delta.text: Clean build. The f

ix is in place.

## Report

**Root cause** — `crates/lua-types/src/value.rs:266
-289` (pre-fix). The weak-table sweep used `weak
ly_held_alone(v)` which returned true only when `Rc::strong_count(v) == 1`. For an entry that
stores the *same* `Rc` in both key and value slots (e.g. `for i=1,l
im do local t={}; a[t]=t end`), the entry itself contributes 2 strong refs to `t`, so `str
ong_count == 2` even with no external refs, and the entry was incorrectly kept in `__mode='kv'` tables. The
failing line was `assert(i == 4)` at gc.

lua:299 — the table actually held ~19 entries instead of 4.

**Fix** — Rewrote `entry_is_we
akly_dead` to call a new `is_dead_side(target, k, v, weak_k, weak_v
)` that (a) computes how many of the entry's slots reference `target` via `Rc::ptr_eq` and (b
) honors which slots are "strong" per the table's mode: in `__mode='v
'`, the key slot is strong (so `a[t]=t` survives), but in `__mode='kv'` both
slots are weak (so `a[t]=t` is collected when there are no other refs). Added `strong_count_of` and `same_

rc` helpers.

**New state** — `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green. Per the instructions I did not re-run the program
; the mega-loop will re-test. No temporary instrumentation was added (the fix was deduced from the data
structure and test source).

content.text:
Clean build. The fix is in place.

## Report

**Root cause** — `crates/lua-types/src/value.rs:266-289` (pre-fix). The weak-table sweep used `weakly_held_alone(v)` which returned true only when `Rc::strong_count(v) == 1`. For an entry that stores the *same* `Rc` in both key and value slots (e.g. `for i=1,lim do local t={}; a[t]=t end`), the entry itself contributes 2 strong refs to `t`, so `strong_count == 2` even with no external refs, and the entry was incorrectly kept in `__mode='kv'` tables. The failing line was `assert(i == 4)` at gc.lua:299 — the table actually held ~19 entries instead of 4.

**Fix** — Rewrote `entry_is_weakly_dead` to call a new `is_dead_side(target, k, v, weak_k, weak_v)` that (a) computes how many of the entry's slots reference `target` via `Rc::ptr_eq` and (b) honors which slots are "strong" per the table's mode: in `__mode='v'`, the key slot is strong (so `a[t]=t` survives), but in `__mode='kv'` both slots are weak (so `a[t]=t` is collected when there are no other refs). Added `strong_count_of` and `same_rc` helpers.

**New state** — `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green. Per the instructions I did not re-run the program; the mega-loop will re-test. No temporary instrumentation was added (the fix was deduced from the data structure and test source).

result:
Clean build. The fix is in place.

## Report

**Root cause** — `crates/lua-types/src/value.rs:266-289` (pre-fix). The weak-table sweep used `weakly_held_alone(v)` which returned true only when `Rc::strong_count(v) == 1`. For an entry that stores the *same* `Rc` in both key and value slots (e.g. `for i=1,lim do local t={}; a[t]=t end`), the entry itself contributes 2 strong refs to `t`, so `strong_count == 2` even with no external refs, and the entry was incorrectly kept in `__mode='kv'` tables. The failing line was `assert(i == 4)` at gc.lua:299 — the table actually held ~19 entries instead of 4.

**Fix** — Rewrote `entry_is_weakly_dead` to call a new `is_dead_side(target, k, v, weak_k, weak_v)` that (a) computes how many of the entry's slots reference `target` via `Rc::ptr_eq` and (b) honors which slots are "strong" per the table's mode: in `__mode='v'`, the key slot is strong (so `a[t]=t` survives), but in `__mode='kv'` both slots are weak (so `a[t]=t` is collected when there are no other refs). Added `strong_count_of` and `same_rc` helpers.

**New state** — `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green. Per the instructions I did not re-run the program; the mega-loop will re-test. No temporary instrumentation was added (the fix was deduced from the data structure and test source).
